body.content: In her seminal Disney paper, “Painterly Rendering for Animation” (SIGGRAPH ’96), Barbara Meier presents a ground-breaking idea: why not use particles, instead of triangles, to render brush strokes? Since then, many similar approaches have been proposed for artistic, non-photorealistic rendering (NPR), trying to tackle the (challenging) problem of placing and orienting those particle-brushes while keeping temporal coherence.

In this little demo, I use (mostly temporally coherent) information from the depth buffer and object UV coordinates to compute particle sizes, colors and orientations. It runs interactively in the browser even for 100k particles!
